audit.scope: full-code review findings gathered from repeated audit passes

verification.pytest -q: failed locally with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'orchestrator'

audit.note: Issues include confirmed bugs, edge-case bugs, design risks, packaging/deployment mismatches, and tests that currently encode risky behavior.

issues.title: Docker CMD passes --base-dir to orchestrator CLI, but the CLI does not define that option
issues.evidence: CMD uses python -m orchestrator --web --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8765 --base-dir /data/runs, but orchestrator.__main__.parse_args has no --base-dir.
issues.impact: The documented Docker container exits with argparse error instead of starting the web UI.

issues.title: README documents --base-dir for python -m orchestrator --web, but that entrypoint does not support it
issues.evidence: README says outputs are created under --base-dir, while --base-dir only exists in orchestrator.webui main, not the top-level --web path.
issues.impact: Users following the README hit CLI errors or cannot configure the web run directory through the documented command.

issues.title: --max-units can leave unprocessed units in designed state while the run exits successfully
issues.evidence: The scheduler adds all architect units to the board, then slices unit_list with max_units. Reproduction showed rc=0 with U1 done and U2/U3 still designed.
issues.impact: Partial runs can be reported as successful even though known work units were never built or tested.

issues.title: CLI success/failure summary ignores incomplete non-terminal unit states
issues.evidence: Exit code checks only failed/blocked units and warnings. It does not fail on designed, in_progress, testing, or dev_done units.
issues.impact: Automation can treat incomplete runs as successful.

issues.title: Final cleanup does not mark designed units as failed or skipped
issues.evidence: Cleanup only handles IN_PROGRESS, DEV_DONE, and TESTING, explicitly leaving DESIGNED alone.
issues.impact: Units that were never started remain in a non-terminal state without making the run fail.

issues.title: Unknown dependencies are warned and ignored instead of blocking the dependent unit
issues.evidence: _wait_for_deps filters unknown deps out and returns true if no known deps remain.
issues.impact: Architect dependency typos can cause units to run before required work exists.

issues.title: Dependency stall detection uses all agent activity, not activity related to the dependency
issues.evidence: The progress signature uses max(updated_at) across all agents.
issues.impact: Unrelated PM/PL or other agent activity can reset dependency idle time and delay or prevent stall failure.

issues.title: Unit IDs are embedded directly into result paths without slug or path validation
issues.evidence: result_rel = f".orchestrator/results/{role}__{key}.json" where key is unit['id']. Values containing ../ can resolve outside results.
issues.impact: Malformed or adversarial architect output can write or delete result files outside the intended result directory.

issues.title: Board normalizes unit IDs to strings but does not restrict separators or path traversal tokens
issues.evidence: uid is str(raw_id).strip(); no validation against '/', '..', whitespace, or invalid identifier characters.
issues.impact: The same unsafe unit ID flows into filenames, generated code, migrations, reports, and artifact paths.

issues.title: Mock backend uses unit IDs directly in file paths and code identifiers
issues.evidence: Mock writes frontend/src/{unit['id']}.jsx and generates function names from unit IDs.
issues.impact: Unexpected unit IDs can create nested paths or invalid generated code even in mock validation runs.

issues.title: Runner accepts agent artifact paths without existence or project-boundary validation
issues.evidence: _coerce_result converts artifacts to strings only.
issues.impact: Reports and UI can list absolute paths, ../ paths, or files that were never created.

issues.title: expose_team_agents overwrites target .claude/agents files despite scaffold being described as non-destructive
issues.evidence: Each bundled agent file is written to dest_dir unconditionally.
issues.impact: Existing custom target subagents can be overwritten.

issues.title: Web stop deletes run.pid immediately before the process is actually dead
issues.evidence: stop sends SIGTERM, schedules SIGKILL later, then unlinks pidfile immediately.
issues.impact: UI and monitor can show stopped while the process continues writing run state.

issues.title: TUI rerun can launch a new process for the same project directory when pidfile state is stale or prematurely removed
issues.evidence: _rerun only checks _run_alive via run.pid, then starts the saved argv in the same project-dir.
issues.impact: Two orchestrator processes can write the same .orchestrator files concurrently.

issues.title: RunManager keeps completed processes in _procs
issues.evidence: is_running reaps and closes logfile but never removes the run_id from self._procs.
issues.impact: Long-lived web servers accumulate stale process objects.

issues.title: OpenAI Agents run_bash uses shell=True and only sets cwd, without enforcing a filesystem boundary
issues.evidence: The function tool executes arbitrary shell commands with cwd=root. The comment acknowledges shell does not enforce the FS boundary.
issues.impact: Bash-capable roles can read or affect files outside the target project.

issues.title: Subprocess timeout kills immediately with SIGKILL and offers no SIGTERM grace period
issues.evidence: Timeout handler calls os.killpg(..., SIGKILL) directly.
issues.impact: Backends cannot clean up locks, temp files, or partial state gracefully.

issues.title: Package data excludes bundled agents, templates, examples, and docs required by runtime behavior
issues.evidence: Only orchestrator/codex_pricing.json is configured as package data; FRAMEWORK_ROOT expects .claude/agents and templates at repository root.
issues.impact: A normal wheel install can miss files needed for scaffolding and role loading.

issues.title: Plain pytest -q fails locally unless the project is installed or invoked as python -m pytest
issues.evidence: Observed pytest -q failure: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'orchestrator'.
issues.impact: Local contributor workflow differs from CI and from python3 -m pytest behavior.

issues.title: ruff format --check fails on tests/test_hardening.py
issues.evidence: ruff format --check . reports: Would reformat: tests/test_hardening.py.
issues.impact: The current CI workflow will fail at the formatting step.

issues.title: run_subprocess timeout only wraps stream pumping, not the final proc.wait
issues.evidence: asyncio.wait_for wraps gather(_pump stdout, _pump stderr), then await proc.wait() is outside the timeout.
issues.impact: A process that closes stdout/stderr but keeps running can bypass the timeout.

issues.title: SIGTERM/SIGINT handler only sets the supervisor stop event and does not cancel active build tasks
issues.evidence: Signal handler calls self._stop.set; build/test/cicd paths do not consult it before continuing.
issues.impact: Graceful shutdown is incomplete; long role calls can continue until externally killed.

issues.title: Web stop relies on forced SIGKILL because the scheduler does not fully honor graceful stop
issues.evidence: stop sends SIGTERM and schedules SIGKILL after 4 seconds.
issues.impact: Stopping from the web UI can interrupt writes and leave partial artifacts.

issues.title: TUI stop has the same SIGTERM then SIGKILL behavior as web stop
issues.evidence: _stop_run sends SIGTERM and schedules SIGKILL after 4 seconds.
issues.impact: Stopping from the monitor can interrupt writes and leave partial artifacts.

issues.title: Runner deletes result_path before each backend attempt without first proving it is under the results directory
issues.evidence: if result_path.exists(): result_path.unlink(). result_path includes unvalidated unit IDs.
issues.impact: Unsafe unit IDs can cause deletion of files outside the intended result namespace.

issues.title: Per-unit artifacts are stored without path validation
issues.evidence: add_artifacts appends artifact strings directly to unit state.
issues.impact: Reports and UI can display misleading or unsafe artifact paths.

issues.title: Global artifacts are stored without path validation
issues.evidence: add_global_artifacts appends artifact strings directly to board state.
issues.impact: Design/docs/cicd artifact lists can include paths outside the target project or paths that do not exist.

issues.title: Deliverables unit headings do not escape unit IDs or titles
issues.evidence: unit_files emits f"### {u['id']} — {u.get('title', '')}" directly.
issues.impact: LLM-provided titles can break Markdown structure.

issues.title: Artifact entries in deliverables are not escaped
issues.evidence: unit_files emits f"- {a}" directly.
issues.impact: Artifact names containing newlines or Markdown syntax can inject unintended document content.

issues.title: Supervisor directives are appended to Markdown without size or content controls
issues.evidence: append_directive writes raw text to directives.md.
issues.impact: Large or malformed LLM output can bloat or distort directives.md.

issues.title: Runner logs full prompts, including spec and directives, to per-agent logs
issues.evidence: write_agent_block logs [SYSTEM] and [TASK] prompt bodies.
issues.impact: Sensitive information in specs or directives is persisted in .orchestrator/agents logs.

issues.title: Runner logs full backend result messages without truncation
issues.evidence: write_agent_block writes res.final_message or res.error with no size cap.
issues.impact: Large backend outputs can produce very large log files and expose sensitive data.

issues.title: write_agent_block has no maximum body size
issues.evidence: The method writes the full body string directly to the agent log.
issues.impact: A single prompt or result can grow logs significantly.

issues.title: Web UI reads up to 600 log lines per role on every state request
issues.evidence: _read_agent_logs reads tail lines for every role.
issues.impact: Large logs can make /api/state slow and heavy.

issues.title: /api/state returns all role logs even though an agent-specific endpoint exists
issues.evidence: agent_logs = _read_agent_logs(orch, list(ROLES)) is included in every /api/state response.
issues.impact: Dashboard polling transfers unnecessary data every second.

issues.title: TUI detail view reads up to 2000 log lines every refresh
issues.evidence: _draw_detail calls _read_agent_log(orch_dir, role, n=2000) on each redraw.
issues.impact: Large logs can make the terminal UI sluggish.

issues.title: RunConfig integer normalization raises raw ValueError for invalid programmatic input
issues.evidence: __post_init__ calls int(...) directly.
issues.impact: Library callers get unstructured exceptions instead of validation errors.

issues.title: build_command can raise from corrupted saved options and expose raw exception text through rerun
issues.evidence: build_command int() conversions can fail; /api/rerun returns str(e).
issues.impact: Malformed _run_opts.json creates user-facing internal error strings.

issues.title: rerun.json stores raw command arguments
issues.evidence: rerun.json writes sys.argv[1:].
issues.impact: Local paths or sensitive argument values persist in run artifacts.

issues.title: Saved rerun argv may contain relative paths that break when rerun from a different cwd
issues.evidence: The scheduler stores raw argv instead of normalized absolute spec/project paths.
issues.impact: TUI rerun can fail or target the wrong path depending on the current working directory.

issues.title: backend_status does not isolate exceptions from backend availability checks
issues.evidence: The loop calls b.available() without try/except.
issues.impact: One broken backend check can break --check, /api/check, and TUI backend display.

issues.title: Claude CLI backend truncates stderr to 500 characters
issues.evidence: Error result uses err.decode(... )[:500].
issues.impact: Important authentication or permission details can be omitted.

issues.title: Codex CLI backend truncates stderr to 500 characters
issues.evidence: Error result uses err.decode(... )[:500].
issues.impact: Important sandbox, login, or runtime diagnostics can be omitted.

issues.title: Claude team backend truncates stderr to 500 characters
issues.evidence: Error result uses err.decode(... )[:500].
issues.impact: Important subagent dispatch or CLI diagnostics can be omitted.

issues.title: Claude SDK backend does not populate model or token usage
issues.evidence: State captures final and cost only.
issues.impact: UI cost/token/model display is less complete for SDK runs.

issues.title: OpenAI Agents backend does not populate cost, model, or token usage
issues.evidence: Returns RoleResult(ok=True, final_message=...) only.
issues.impact: Backend comparisons and budget visibility are inconsistent.

issues.title: Completion report prompt does not define strict status enum or path/id restrictions
issues.evidence: Schema shows generic status/artifacts/units fields without allowed values or safe naming rules.
issues.impact: Agents can produce values that later require defensive handling but are currently undervalidated.

issues.title: Prompt says most roles must not install dependencies, but runtime tool permissions do not enforce that
issues.evidence: Roles with Bash can still run install commands; this is policy-by-prompt only.
issues.impact: Real backends can mutate environments despite prompt guidance.

issues.title: Project manager supervisor has Bash access
issues.evidence: tools: Read, Bash while the role states it does not write code.
issues.impact: A supervisor role can execute shell commands despite being conceptually read-only.

issues.title: Project leader supervisor has Bash access
issues.evidence: tools: Read, Bash.
issues.impact: A supervisor role can execute shell commands despite being conceptually review-only.

issues.title: OpenAI Agents optional dependency may be brittle across environments
issues.evidence: openai optional dependency is openai-agents>=0.0.1; Docker later masks optional install failures.
issues.impact: Users may believe OpenAI backend is installed when fallback installation dropped the extra.

issues.title: Docker build masks optional backend dependency installation failures
issues.evidence: RUN pip install -e ".[all]" || pip install -e .
issues.impact: Images can build successfully without requested real backend dependencies.

issues.title: Docker image does not copy README.en.md, docs, or tests
issues.evidence: Dockerfile copies pyproject.toml, README.md, orchestrator, .claude, templates, and examples only.
issues.impact: Container runtime lacks some project documentation and local verification assets.

issues.title: README says CI runs lint and tests, but current formatting check fails
issues.evidence: README points to CI workflow; local ruff format --check fails.
issues.impact: Documentation overstates current CI readiness.

issues.title: CI workflow will fail because formatting is not currently clean
issues.evidence: CI runs ruff format --check ., and local check reports tests/test_hardening.py would be reformatted.
issues.impact: Pull requests or pushes fail CI until formatting is fixed.

issues.title: CLI tests do not assert nonzero exit code for failed, blocked, or incomplete runs
issues.evidence: test_mock_run_returns_zero covers only a successful mock run.
issues.impact: Exit-code regressions and incomplete-run success bugs can escape tests.

issues.title: Web stop test encodes immediate pidfile deletion behavior
issues.evidence: The test asserts run.pid does not exist immediately after stop.
issues.impact: A safer stop implementation would require changing this test.

issues.title: Unknown dependency test encodes the current warn-and-ignore behavior
issues.evidence: The test asserts _wait_for_deps returns True when the only dependency is unknown.
issues.impact: Changing unknown dependencies to hard failures requires updating this test.

issues.title: Numeric option policy differs between web validation and RunConfig normalization
issues.evidence: Web rejects 0/negative values with 400, while RunConfig clamps them.
issues.impact: Different entrypoints behave differently for the same invalid settings.

issues.title: MAX_SPEC_BYTES name does not match implementation because spec size is checked by character count after JSON parsing
issues.evidence: The code checks len(data.get('spec_text', '')), not encoded byte length.
issues.impact: Multibyte specs can exceed the intended byte limit while passing the character-count check.

issues.title: Web-launched runs hardcode poll interval to 600 seconds
issues.evidence: build_command always appends --poll-interval 600, while the CLI default is 20 seconds and the web UI exposes no control.
issues.impact: PM/PL supervision cadence differs silently between CLI and web runs.

issues.title: Web UI cannot pass timeout, retries, budget, or model options to orchestrator runs
issues.evidence: build_command forwards backend, backends, role_backends, distribute, cross_check, mock, delegate, max_units, and max_attempts only.
issues.impact: Real backend runs started from the browser cannot use important CLI controls documented for production runs.

issues.title: Run button can remain disabled if startRun fetch or JSON parsing throws
issues.evidence: startRun disables runBtn, awaits fetch and r.json, then re-enables it without try/finally.
issues.impact: A network error or non-JSON response can leave the launch UI stuck until refresh.

issues.title: Dashboard polling can overlap because loop does not await tick
issues.evidence: loop awaits refreshRuns but calls tick() without await every second.
issues.impact: Slow /api/state responses can race each other and render stale state after newer state.

issues.title: Backend status panel renders backend names and reasons via innerHTML without escaping
issues.evidence: loadChecks concatenates r.name and r.reason directly into innerHTML.
issues.impact: If an availability reason ever contains HTML, the browser can render unintended markup or script.

issues.title: Run picker builds inline onclick handlers from run IDs
issues.evidence: renderPicker injects onclick="selectRun('" + r.id + "')" into innerHTML. list_runs uses directory names under base_dir, not only new_run_id outputs.
issues.impact: A manually created run directory with quotes or script-like content can break markup or inject JavaScript.

issues.title: RunManager.project_dir accepts empty run IDs as the base directory itself
issues.evidence: project_dir allows p == base. An empty run_id resolves to base rather than being rejected.
issues.impact: APIs can operate against base_dir/.orchestrator for invalid or missing run IDs.

issues.title: /api/agent does not require a non-empty run or role
issues.evidence: do_GET for /api/agent reads run and role defaults as empty strings and still calls manager.project_dir(run).
issues.impact: Invalid agent requests return empty successful responses instead of clear 400 or 404 errors.

issues.title: /api/state returns an empty successful state for nonexistent runs
issues.evidence: If the requested run directory has no board.json, _read_board returns {}, and the API still returns 200.
issues.impact: The UI and callers cannot distinguish a run that has not initialized yet from a nonexistent or broken run.

issues.title: Corrupt board.json is silently treated as an empty board
issues.evidence: _read_board catches all exceptions from json.loads and returns {}.
issues.impact: State corruption is hidden from the web UI and TUI instead of being surfaced as a run error.

issues.title: Cross-check warning ignores role-pinned backends that can make a two-backend pool
issues.evidence: build_config warns whenever --cross-check has fewer than two --backends, but RunConfig._cross_pool can add role_priority values to the pool.
issues.impact: Users can receive a warning that cross-check will not apply even when role pins make it apply.

issues.title: Mock checkbox is checked by default and silently overrides selected real backends
issues.evidence: The browser sends mock:true by default. RunConfig.backends_for returns mock before considering backend selections.
issues.impact: A user can select a real backend in the web UI but still get a mock run unless they notice and uncheck mock.

issues.title: docs/PLAN.md contains stale implementation claims
issues.evidence: The plan mentions board MCP tools and command-injection prevention for OpenAI bash, while the current implementation uses result files and documents that run_bash only sets cwd.
issues.impact: Maintainers can be misled about actual security and architecture behavior.

issues.title: docs/architecture.html depends on a remote CDN to render Mermaid
issues.evidence: The page imports mermaid from https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/...
issues.impact: The architecture diagram does not render offline or in restricted network environments.

issues.title: docs/architecture.html describes stale runtime details
issues.evidence: The diagram mentions board MCP tools and ClaudeSDKClient supervisor loops, which do not match the current baseline implementation.
issues.impact: Architecture documentation can lead readers to believe unsupported runtime mechanisms exist.

issues.title: Target templates document result file naming without spelling out global vs unit keys
issues.evidence: The template says .orchestrator/results/<role>__<unit>.json, while non-unit roles use global and supervisors do not require result files.
issues.impact: Agents or humans reading target instructions can misunderstand which result file to write.

issues.title: AGENTS.md template uses a malformed JSON schema example
issues.evidence: The inline example is {"status","artifacts":[],"notes":[],"blockers":[],"units":[]}, which is not valid JSON.
issues.impact: Codex-style agents that rely on AGENTS.md may copy an invalid result schema.

issues.title: POST handlers assume parsed JSON is an object
issues.evidence: json.loads can return a list, string, number, or null. Later code calls data.get(...) unconditionally.
issues.impact: Malformed but valid JSON bodies can raise AttributeError and drop the HTTP connection instead of returning 400.

issues.title: backends option type is not validated
issues.evidence: The code iterates data.get('backends') directly. If a client sends a string, it iterates characters rather than backend names.
issues.impact: API behavior for malformed clients is confusing and can produce misleading validation errors.

issues.title: role_backends option type is not validated before .items()
issues.evidence: The code calls (data.get('role_backends') or {}).items() without checking that role_backends is a dict.
issues.impact: Malformed clients can crash the request handler instead of receiving a structured 400 response.

issues.title: list_runs can surface symlinked or externally moved run directories inconsistently
issues.evidence: list_runs enumerates base_dir entries by path name, while RunManager.project_dir later resolves and rejects paths outside base_dir.
issues.impact: A symlinked run can appear in /api/runs but later fail state/stop/rerun operations.

issues.title: set_status logs success even when the target unit ID does not exist
issues.evidence: set_status loops through units and flushes, but does not track whether a unit was actually updated before logging status=...
issues.impact: Events can claim a status transition happened when board state did not change.

issues.title: set_test_status silently no-ops for unknown unit IDs
issues.evidence: set_test_status does not report or warn when no matching unit is found.
issues.impact: Test result updates can be lost without any warning.

issues.title: add_artifacts silently drops artifacts for unknown unit IDs
issues.evidence: add_artifacts loops through units and appends only on match, with no warning if no unit matches.
issues.impact: Artifacts from a role run can disappear from reports if the unit ID mismatches.

issues.title: add_cost accepts non-finite float values
issues.evidence: add_cost converts amount with float(amount) and json.dumps later writes NaN or Infinity by default if present.
issues.impact: A malformed backend cost can produce non-standard JSON in board.json.

issues.title: agent_update can raise on invalid token or cost inputs
issues.evidence: cost_add and tokens_add are converted with float() and int() without local error handling.
issues.impact: Unexpected backend metadata can break a board update path.

issues.title: Board log paths are not sanitized when role is passed programmatically
issues.evidence: _append_agent_log and write_agent_block use f'{role}.log' directly.
issues.impact: Internal callers using untrusted role names could write logs outside the agents directory.

issues.title: _send does not handle BrokenPipeError or client disconnects
issues.evidence: _send writes body directly to self.wfile without catching socket write errors.
issues.impact: Client disconnects can produce noisy handler tracebacks.

issues.title: spec_text type is not validated before writing to disk
issues.evidence: The handler uses len(data.get('spec_text', '') or '') and passes the value to RunManager.start, which calls Path.write_text and expects a string.
issues.impact: A valid JSON body with spec_text as a list/object can crash the request handler.

issues.title: TUI rerun trusts mutable rerun.json arguments
issues.evidence: _rerun reads argv from .orchestrator/rerun.json and launches python -m orchestrator with those arguments.
issues.impact: If rerun.json is edited or corrupted, the monitor can launch an unexpected orchestrator command.

issues.title: scaffold overwrites .orchestrator/spec.md on existing target projects
issues.evidence: scaffold always writes spec_text to .orchestrator/spec.md.
issues.impact: Existing run metadata in a reused project directory can be overwritten.

issues.title: gitignore seeding treats any .orchestrator/ substring as already configured
issues.evidence: The check is if '.orchestrator/' not in cur, which also matches comments or unrelated patterns.
issues.impact: A target .gitignore with a commented mention can fail to ignore actual orchestrator state.

issues.title: Main role frontmatter model is parsed but ignored
issues.evidence: load_agent returns agent.model, but _build_req sets model from cfg.model_for(backend_name) only. agent.model is used only for delegated teammates.
issues.impact: Per-agent model choices in .claude/agents/*.md do not affect normal role execution.

issues.title: model: inherit can be passed as a literal delegated teammate model
issues.evidence: _build_teammates includes td.model directly. The bundled agent files set model: inherit.
issues.impact: Delegation backends may receive 'inherit' as an actual model name instead of treating it as unspecified.

issues.title: OpenAI Agents Edit tool is implemented as full-file overwrite
issues.evidence: tool_map maps both Write and Edit to write_file.
issues.impact: Agents expecting an edit operation may overwrite whole files instead of applying targeted changes.

issues.title: Board.units returns shallow copies with mutable nested lists still shared
issues.evidence: units() returns [dict(u)] but nested lists like artifacts, deps, roles, and notes are the same list objects.
issues.impact: Internal callers can mutate board state outside the board lock and without flushing to disk.

issues.title: Result status validation is not role or phase specific
issues.evidence: _SUCCESS_STATUSES accepts generic values such as done, passed, success, and completed for every role.
issues.impact: A dev role can report passed or an architect can report done and still be treated as _ok even if the phase-specific contract was not satisfied.

issues.title: Architect success does not require a non-empty units array
issues.evidence: The scheduler only reads arch_outcome.get('units') or [] and falls back to a core unit when none exist.
issues.impact: An architect result can be considered successful while failing its most important contract: decomposing the spec into units.

issues.title: Web form masks invalid numeric values before server validation
issues.evidence: startRun sends +concurrency || 3 and +maxAttempts || 2. Values such as 0, NaN, or non-numeric strings become defaults.
issues.impact: The server-side validation for invalid numeric inputs is bypassed for normal browser use.

issues.title: Web role backend controls cannot express per-role priority lists
issues.evidence: The UI builds role_backends as role -> single select value, while CLI supports ROLE=B1[,B2,...].
issues.impact: Browser-launched runs cannot configure per-role failover priority even though the CLI and config model support it.

issues.title: Web API accepts only scalar role_backends values but build_command does not handle list priorities
issues.evidence: build_command emits f'{role}={prov}'. If API clients send a list to represent priority, the list repr is passed to the CLI as one malformed backend string.
issues.impact: The API shape cannot faithfully represent the CLI's role-backend priority feature.

issues.title: Top-level --web repurposes --project-dir as the web run base directory
issues.evidence: main calls serve(a.port, a.project_dir, a.host) when --web is set, although --project-dir is documented as the target output directory for a single run.
issues.impact: The same option has different meanings between CLI run mode and web server mode, which can confuse users and scripts.

issues.title: write_report reports ok for an empty board with no units and no warnings
issues.evidence: If units is empty and warnings is empty, result is set to ok.
issues.impact: A report generated before unit creation or after state loss can look successful despite containing no work.

issues.title: README examples use --max-units in real mode even though current max-units behavior can produce partial-success reports
issues.evidence: The real-mode demo uses --max-units 2, while the scheduler currently leaves unprocessed units in designed state and can still exit 0.
issues.impact: The documented demo path exercises a known partial-run success bug.

issues.title: Ignored cache and OS files are present in the working tree
issues.evidence: find shows .DS_Store, .pytest_cache, .ruff_cache, and __pycache__ files in the repository directory.
issues.impact: They are ignored, but they add local noise and can confuse file audits or packaging checks.

issues.title: Docker deployment binds the unauthenticated web UI to 0.0.0.0 by default
issues.evidence: The Docker CMD starts --web --host 0.0.0.0. README notes the web UI has no auth.
issues.impact: Running the documented container command can expose run controls on any reachable network interface unless the operator adds external protection.

issues.title: Valid JSON result files that are not objects can be accepted as successful
issues.evidence: _coerce_result replaces non-dict JSON values with {}, then defaults status to done when the backend returned ok=True.
issues.impact: A role can write [] or "done" instead of the required object schema and still be treated as _ok.

issues.title: Codex backend output path embeds unit IDs without path validation
issues.evidence: out_path is built from f'{req.role}__{key}__{uuid}.codex.txt' where key is req.unit['id'].
issues.impact: Unsafe unit IDs can move Codex transcript output outside the intended .orchestrator/results directory.

issues.title: Claude CLI backend reports ready when the binary exists, without verifying login or API credentials
issues.evidence: available() only checks shutil.which('claude') and returns ready (login subscription or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY).
issues.impact: --check and the web backend panel can show Claude CLI as ready even though the first real role call will fail for auth.

issues.title: Claude team backend reports ready when the binary exists, without verifying login or API credentials
issues.evidence: available() only checks shutil.which('claude').
issues.impact: The native subagent backend can appear available while actual dispatch fails for authentication.

issues.title: Codex backend reports ready when the binary exists, without verifying login or API credentials
issues.evidence: available() only checks shutil.which('codex') and returns ready (codex login or CODEX_API_KEY).
issues.impact: --check and the web backend panel can show Codex as ready even though real execution fails for auth.

issues.title: Budget option is only enforced by Claude SDK but is accepted globally
issues.evidence: RoleRequest carries budget for all backends, but Claude CLI, Claude team, Codex, OpenAI Agents, and mock do not enforce it.
issues.impact: Users can pass --budget and assume cost protection while most backend paths ignore it.

issues.title: Claude SDK option compatibility fallback can silently drop the budget cap
issues.evidence: _make_options catches TypeError and removes max_budget_usd among other options before retrying.
issues.impact: If the installed SDK does not accept the budget option, the run proceeds without the requested budget limit and without surfacing that fact.

issues.title: OpenAI Agents backend ignores RoleRequest.budget
issues.evidence: Agent kwargs include name, instructions, tools, and optional model only; req.budget is never used.
issues.impact: OpenAI Agents runs have no budget cap despite the global CLI accepting --budget.

issues.title: Codex backend ignores RoleRequest.budget
issues.evidence: The codex command includes sandbox/json/output/model flags but no budget-related behavior.
issues.impact: Codex runs can exceed the user's intended budget.

issues.title: Claude CLI and Claude team backends ignore RoleRequest.budget
issues.evidence: The Claude CLI command does not include or enforce any budget cap; claude_team uses the same CLI pattern.
issues.impact: Subscription/API CLI runs can proceed without budget enforcement even when --budget was provided.

issues.title: Claude CLI backend ignores RoleRequest.max_turns
issues.evidence: RoleRequest carries max_turns, but the claude command is built without any turn-limit option.
issues.impact: The documented per-session max_turns safety rail is not enforced for Claude CLI runs.

issues.title: Claude team backend ignores RoleRequest.max_turns
issues.evidence: The claude-team command is built without a max-turns equivalent even though RoleRequest includes max_turns.
issues.impact: Native subagent dispatch runs do not enforce the same turn cap as SDK-backed runs.

issues.title: Codex backend ignores RoleRequest.max_turns
issues.evidence: The codex exec command does not use req.max_turns.
issues.impact: Codex runs are not bounded by the role phase max_turns configured in the orchestrator.

issues.title: Build pipeline gather does not use return_exceptions and can skip final unit cleanup on internal exceptions
issues.evidence: asyncio.gather(*[pipeline(u) for u in unit_list]) uses the default exception behavior. If a pipeline raises, the cleanup block that marks non-terminal units failed is skipped.
issues.impact: An unexpected internal exception can leave units in in_progress/designed states and produce incomplete reports.

issues.title: Development role gather assumes runner.run_role never raises
issues.evidence: _develop_unit awaits asyncio.gather over dev role calls without return_exceptions or a local wrapper.
issues.impact: A regression in Runner or a programmatic monkeypatch can abort the unit pipeline instead of marking the unit failed.

issues.title: OpenAI Agents read_file has no size limit
issues.evidence: read_file returns the entire file contents.
issues.impact: Agents can accidentally load very large files into tool output and context.

issues.title: OpenAI Agents write_file has no content size limit
issues.evidence: write_file writes the full provided content string without a maximum size.
issues.impact: A malformed tool call can create very large files under the target project.

issues.title: OpenAI Agents run_bash output omits the command exit code
issues.evidence: run_bash returns stdout+stderr truncated to 4000 characters but does not include r.returncode.
issues.impact: The agent may misinterpret a failed command as successful if the textual output is ambiguous.

issues.title: Mock CI workflow YAML is malformed
issues.evidence: The generated ci.yml string concatenates runs-on and steps without a newline between ubuntu-latest and steps.
issues.impact: A mock run can produce an invalid .github/workflows/ci.yml while tests only assert the file exists.

issues.title: Mock frontend component name is derived directly from unit ID and can be invalid JavaScript
issues.evidence: The generated JSX uses export default function {unit['id']}(), but IDs such as U-1, 1, or A/B are not valid function identifiers.
issues.impact: Mock validation can generate syntactically invalid frontend files.

issues.title: Mock test function name is derived directly from unit ID and can be invalid Python
issues.evidence: The generated test uses def test_{unit['id'].lower()}(): without sanitizing the ID.
issues.impact: Mock validation can generate syntactically invalid pytest files.

issues.title: Mock database table name is derived directly from unit ID and can be invalid SQL
issues.evidence: The generated migration uses CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS t_{unit['id'].lower()} without identifier quoting or sanitization.
issues.impact: Mock validation can generate invalid SQL for non-simple unit IDs.

issues.title: Mock E2E sheet ignores the provided spec text
issues.evidence: _mock_e2e accepts spec_text but returns a fixed smoke scenario.
issues.impact: Mock testsheet output can look successful while not reflecting the actual uploaded spec.

issues.title: CI/CD and docs phases still run after failed or blocked units
issues.evidence: After build/test cleanup, the scheduler always enters cicd and docs phases regardless of failed or blocked units.
issues.impact: Later phases can produce deliverables for an incomplete or failed build, making artifacts look more complete than they are.

issues.title: Final phase is set to done even when units or post-build phases failed
issues.evidence: The scheduler sets phase to done after finishing supervisors regardless of warnings or failed units.
issues.impact: Consumers that look only at phase can interpret a failed run as completed successfully.

issues.title: Monitor state label does not include failed or blocked unit status
issues.evidence: _state_label only considers alive, phase, and warnings. It does not inspect failed or blocked units.
issues.impact: The TUI and one-shot snapshot can show done or stopped without making failed units obvious in the run state label.

issues.title: Backend availability view can overflow vertically without scrolling
issues.evidence: _draw_backends writes one row per backend and does not guard against terminal height except through _safe_add silently dropping rows.
issues.impact: On small terminals some backend status rows can disappear with no scroll path.
